papers.title_en: High-Fidelity 4D Hand-Object Capture via Multi-View Spatiotemporal Tracking and Physics-Aware Gaussians
papers.authors: Bo Peng, Xu Chen, Yi Gu, Hidenobu Matsuki, Mingsong Dou, Jingjing Shen, Deying Kong, Juyong Zhang, Zhengyang Shen
papers.evidence: 多视角时空跟踪手和物体以实现4D捕捉
papers.tldr: 现有4D手物交互数据采集依赖预扫描模板和物理标记，且对初始位姿估计敏感，尤其在严重遮挡下难以鲁棒重建。本文提出无需模板或标记的系统，利用多视角前馈transformer模型聚合跨视角几何和时间线索，为手物位姿和物体形状提供可靠初始化；再通过物理感知高斯优化框架，整合四面体约束、碰撞细化和外观分解，生成物理合理、视觉精确的重建。在公开和内部数据集上验证，该方法实现鲁棒无伪影的4D手物捕捉，为自动化4D资产生成奠定基础。
papers.abstract_en: The growing demand for high-fidelity 4D hand-object interaction (HOI) data in embodied AI and spatial computing is currently bottlenecked by the reliance on pre-scanned object templates and physical markers. While recent methods have demonstrated promising results in reconstructing 4D hand-object interaction from videos, they are highly sensitive to initial estimates of hand and object poses. Yet, estimating these poses from images is challenging, in particular under severe occlusion which is inherent in hand-object interaction scenarios. We propose a novel system for the robust and accurate reconstruction of hands and objects from synchronized and calibrated multi-view videos without requiring any templates or markers. Our system consists of two main components with key innovations: (1) a multi-view feed-forward transformer model that aggregates cross-view geometry and temporal cues to provide a reliable, metric-consistent initialization for both poses and dense object geometry, and (2) a hand-object physics-aware Gaussian-based optimization framework to refine the initial estimates, integrating tetrahedral constraints, collision refinement, and appearance decomposition to produce physically plausible and visually accurate reconstruction. Validated on public benchmarks and an extensive internal dataset, our pipeline achieves highly robust, artifact-free reconstruction, providing an efficient foundation for automated 4D asset generation. Our project page are available at https://zyshen021.github.io/HOSTPG/.

papers.title_en: SA-VIS: Sparse frame Annotations for training Video Instance Segmentation
papers.authors: Edoardo Mello Rella, Ajad Chhatkuli, Shipra Jain, Ender Konukoglu, Luc Van Gool
papers.evidence: 视频实例分割，与视觉目标跟踪相关
papers.tldr: 在线视频实例分割通常依赖密集标注帧训练，成本高昂。本文提出SA-VIS方法，通过Past-frames Feature Propagation模块聚合多帧低维特征，仅需稀疏帧标注即可端到端训练。在YouTube-VIS等数据集上，仅用1/5标注性能仅下降0.4%，在有限标注场景下AP提升超1%。该方法简单有效，弥合了稀疏与密集标注间的精度差距。
papers.abstract_en: Recent online video instance segmentation (VIS) methods have achieved impressive results, thus becoming the preferred approach to segment instances in videos. Despite the resurgence of impressive single image models, the online (or semi-online) VIS approaches outperform single-image models (e.g., based on SAM) by using long sequences of densely annotated frames during training. However,such a training setup of VIS is expensive in the sense of compute as well as dense annotations required. In order to solve these major flaws, we argue that the effective modeling of the instances and their evolution in videos do not require densely annotated frames. To that end, we propose a simple and effective module, called Past-frames Feature Propagation (PFP) which aggregates low-dimensional features from the image encoder of multiple frames. This simple low-compute module provides tremendous learning capability in using sparse video frame labels for end-to-end training. Combined with a light-weight frame-specific Instance Queries, our Sparse frame Annotation VIS (SA-VIS) significantly improves performance over its baseline. Most interestingly, our simple design that avoids complexities effectively bridges the gap in accuracy between training on sparsely and densely annotated video sequences. This translates to a mere 0.4% drop in performance of SA-VIS when using annotations for only 1/5 of the images in the dataset. Empirically, SA-VIS shows strong improvements over the baseline on YouTube-VIS 2019/2021/2022 and Occluded VIS (OVIS) and an over 1% improvement in AP on the state-of-the-art in a limited annotations scenario.
